content.verdict: Amansara in Siem Reap occupies King Norodom Sihanouk's former private guesthouse — a 1962 modernist jewel by Vann Molyvann that Aman has restored to impeccable perfection with 24 suites arranged around two pool courtyards. Private dawn access to Angkor Wat before other tourists arrive, cyclo breakfast tours through the sleeping town, and the legendary Aman service create the definitive Angkor honeymoon experience. This is one of the finest Aman properties in the world.
content.best_room: The Pool Suites are the pinnacle Amansara experience — two-room suites with private pools opening directly from the sliding glass doors, the pool courtyard's calm architecture creating a meditative atmosphere of complete peace. The Garden Suites are the original Vann Molyvann rooms surrounding the historic 1962 pool — smaller but architecturally the most significant, with direct pool access. For honeymooners wanting maximum privacy, the Road to Angkor pool suites in the newer wing have larger private pools.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival & Pool Courtyard Evening
itinerary_7_nights.description: Fly into Siem Reap International Airport and transfer directly to Amansara — 10 minutes through the town. The 1962 architecture and frangipani-shaded courtyard are immediately calming. No check-in desk: your private guide welcomes you by name and shows you to your suite. An evening at leisure in the pool courtyard before tomorrow's extraordinary dawn.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Private Dawn Angkor Wat
itinerary_7_nights.description: 3:45am departure with your dedicated guide and private Amansara vehicle. Arrive at Angkor Wat's Naga causeway in darkness, the temple's five towers materialising slowly against a lightening sky. No other tourists. Sunrise from the reflecting pool — perhaps the most cinematic moment in all of Southeast Asia. Return for breakfast after the crowds arrive.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Angkor Thom & Bayon
itinerary_7_nights.description: A full day exploring the walled city of Angkor Thom: the 54-headed Bayon temple, the Baphuon pyramid, and the Elephant Terrace. Your Amansara guide's depth of knowledge transforms the stones into living history — the Khmer Empire's extraordinary civilisation made comprehensible and moving. Spa afternoon on return.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Cyclo Breakfast Tour
itinerary_7_nights.description: Pre-dawn cyclo (traditional bicycle rickshaw) tour through the Siem Reap morning market — the hotel arranges vintage French-era cyclos for a slow meander through waking neighbourhoods. Stop for fresh bánh mì, iced coffee, and a living portrait of Cambodian morning life before the heat builds. Return for a pool morning.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Ta Prohm & Jungle Temples
itinerary_7_nights.description: Ta Prohm — the 'Tomb Raider temple' — at sunrise with Amansara's early access is among Cambodia's most memorable experiences: vast silk-cotton trees splitting ancient stone walls apart in a slow-motion eruption. Afternoon at the Banteay Srei temple, 25km from Angkor — pink sandstone carvings of extraordinary delicacy and preservation.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Tonle Sap Lake & Khmer Cooking
itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning boat trip on Tonle Sap Lake — Southeast Asia's largest freshwater lake and the food bowl of the Khmer civilisation. The floating villages and enormous water bird population are extraordinary. Return for an afternoon Khmer cooking class at Amansara: learn amok (coconut fish curry), lok lak (pepper beef), and the art of green mango salad.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Final Temple & Farewell Dinner
itinerary_7_nights.description: Final morning at Preah Khan temple — perhaps the most atmospheric of all Angkor's jungle ruins, its galleries stretching into dappled forest with few visitors. Return for an Amansara farewell dinner: private candlelit meal in the historic 1962 dining room, the pool lit by moonlight, the temples a short drive away in the darkness.

caveats: Amansara's small scale (24 suites) means it books out very quickly, particularly for peak season months. Early reservation is essential.
Angkor's entrance fees are charged separately and are substantial; confirm inclusive arrangements with Amansara at booking.
Siem Reap can be very hot (35°C+) from March to May; the temples require significant walking in the heat, and the pool courtyard becomes the afternoon sanctuary.

faqs.question: Good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Amansara is the finest honeymoon hotel in Cambodia and one of the best in Southeast Asia. The private dawn Angkor Wat access is a genuinely exclusive romantic experience available only to Amansara guests. The 24-suite scale ensures total intimacy, and the King Sihanouk guesthouse setting gives the stay a historical resonance that adds meaning to every moment.

faqs.question: Best time to visit?
faqs.answer: November through March is the dry season and most comfortable for temple exploration — temperatures are more manageable and skies clear. December and January are peak season. June through October brings the green season with lush vegetation around the temples and fewer tourists, though afternoon rain is frequent. The temples are extraordinary in any season.

faqs.question: Book in advance?
faqs.answer: Book 9 to 12 months ahead for peak season December-January. Pool Suites are the first category to sell out. Amansara is often booked through Aman's global reservations team or luxury travel specialists. Mention your honeymoon for personalised arrangements including private temple dinners on request.

faqs.question: Adults-only?
faqs.answer: Amansara is not adults-only, though its sophisticated quiet atmosphere, absence of a kids' club, and the contemplative character of temple exploration make it predominantly adult in practice. Families are rare; the guest mix is overwhelmingly couples, including many honeymooners.

faqs.question: Best room type?
faqs.answer: Pool Suites with private pools are the premier romantic choice for honeymooners. The Original Road to Angkor Pool Suites offer the largest private pools. The Garden Suites around the historic 1962 pool are architecturally the most authentic but smaller. Every suite provides the same extraordinary Amansara service and private temple access.

faqs.question: How to get there?
faqs.answer: Siem Reap International Airport receives direct international flights from Bangkok, Singapore, Kuala Lumpur, Ho Chi Minh City, and Phnom Penh. From Europe and North America, connections are typically via Bangkok or Singapore. The Amansara team meets all guests personally at the airport for seamless transfer to the property.
